title: Methane ethane crackers
abstract: A process for producing olefins by cracking paraffins in the presence of methane. In the conventional steam cracking processes for olefin production, steam is used as a diluent in the feed mixture to the thermal cracker. In the processes provided herein, methane replaces steam as a diluent in the feed mixture to the thermal cracker. Replacing steam with methane as a diluent has a potential for cost savings in the construction and operation of a thermal cracking plant for olefin production. In addition, it leads to a much simpler cracking process compared to the conventional steam cracking technology as in the state of the art.
claims: Claims Claims:  What is claimed is: 1. A process for converting paraffins to olefins, comprising: passing a feed stream containing methane and one or more paraffins to a cracker, wherein the feed stream comprises 30 mol to about 95 mol% methane; converting at least a portion of the one or more paraffins to one or more olefins to provide a product stream comprising the one or more olefins and the methane. 2. The process of claim 1, wherein the cracker comprises a plurality of tubes placed inside a fired furnace. 3. The process of claims 1 or 2, wherein the cracker operates adiabatically. 4. The process of claims 1 to 3, further comprising separating at least a majority of the methane from the feed stream prior to passing the feed stream to the cracker. 5. The process of claims 1 to 4, further comprising separating at least a portion of the methane from the product stream and recycling the separated methane to the feed stream prior to passing the feed stream to the cracker. 6. The process of claims 1 to 5, wherein the use of catalysts in the cracker is not required. 7. The process of claims 1 to 6, wherein the cracker contains a catalyst bed. 8. The process of claim 8, wherein the catalyst bed is a fixed bed, fluidized bed or moving bed. 9. The process of claims 1 to 8, wherein the feed stream further comprises less than 15 mol% of one or more non-paraffinic hydrocarbons and/or non-hydrocarbons. 10. The process of claim 9, wherein the non-hydrocarbons comprise nitrogen, helium, steam, or a mixture thereof, and wherein the one or more paraffins comprise ethane, propane, butane, pentane, naphtha or any combinations thereof. 11. The process of claims 1 to 10, wherein methane concentration in the feed stream is about 30 mol% to about 90 mol%. 12. The process of claims 1 to 11, wherein methane concentration in the feed stream is about 50 mol% to about 90 mol%. 13. The process of claims 1 to 12, wherein the feed stream and cracker have no added steam. 14. The process of claims 1 to 13, wherein the feed stream comprises less than 5 mol% steam. 15. The process of claims 1 to 14, wherein the cracker operates at a temperature of about 500Â°C to about 1,000Â°C, and a pressure of about 1 atm to about 30 atm.
